text.title: Rate Limiting Algorithms
text.summary: Fixed-window, sliding-window, and token-bucket algorithms — when to pick each and Python implementations.

rule.statement: Use sliding-window for most API endpoints; use token-bucket for burst-tolerant endpoints (uploads, batch APIs); use fixed-window only for billing/quota where exact counts matter.
rule.rationale: Fixed-window allows 2x burst at window boundary (199 req at 10:59:59 + 100 at 11:00:00). Sliding-window distributes requests fairly. Token-bucket absorbs legitimate bursts.

rule.statement: Use a shared store (Redis/Valkey) for all rate-limit counters; in-process counters (dict, lru_cache) are forbidden for any deployment with more than one replica.
rule.rationale: In-process counters diverge across replicas: each server independently allows the full limit, multiplying effective throughput by the replica count.

section.title: Sliding Window (Redis)

example.code:
import time import redis.asyncio as redis
class SlidingWindowRateLimiter: def __init__(self, limit: int, window_seconds: int, redis_client): self.limit = limit self.window = window_seconds self.redis = redis_client
async def is_allowed(self, key: str) -> bool: now = time.time() pipe = self.redis.pipeline() pipe.zremrangebyscore(key, 0, now - self.window) pipe.zadd(key, {str(now): now}) pipe.zcard(key) pipe.expire(key, self.window) _, _, count, _ = await pipe.execute() return count <= self.limit

section.reference: Full FastAPI middleware using Redis sliding window

rule.statement: Key on the most expensive identity available: api_key > user_id > session > device_id > ip. Never key solely on IP.
rule.rationale: IP-based limiting punishes mobile carriers and corporate NATs sharing a single egress IP; api_key or user_id provides per-account fairness.

rule.statement: Use Redis TIME command (not time.time()) for the sliding-window timestamp to avoid clock skew between app servers.
rule.rationale: App servers' clocks can diverge by seconds; using a central Redis timestamp ensures consistent window boundaries.
